creator: hrs@FreeBSD.org
text:
----Security_Multipart(Sun_Apr__5_02_47_55_2009_971)--
Content-Type: Text/Plain; charset=us-ascii
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit

Patrick Powell <papowell@astart.com> wrote
  in <200903171857.n2HIv0LP054510@astart2.astart.com>:

pa> The GPL Ghostscript Version 8.62 (GS) allows files in the same directory to be
pa> opened and read by PostScript files being processed by GS.
pa>
pa> When GS is used by the LPRng, CUPS, or legacy LPR printing system to process
pa> print files the current directory is set to the 'spool queue' directory.
pa> A carefully crafted PostScript print job could open, read, and print files
pa> submitted by other users.
pa>
pa> While GPL GhostScript has a '-dPARNOID' option that eliminates other security
pa> issues,  it appears that this one has been missed.  In previous versions of
pa> Ghostscript -dPARANOIDSAFER prevented this problem.  However, the current version
pa> of GPL Ghostscript Version 8.62 (GS) allows files in the 'Search path' to be
pa> read.  The current search path is:

 Thank you for the report.  However, I think this is a problem of
 Ghostscript itself, not print/ghostscript8.  Please contact the
 Ghostscript maintainer directly.

 I am wondering if the -dPARANOIDSAFER is defined as an way to
 eliminate the problem you pointed out, and I do not know well about a
 policy of Ghostscript for it.  If you do not want to add the current
 directory into the search path, a "-P-" option can be used.

--
| Hiroki SATO
